title: Democrats’ Bill Would Deny For-Profit College Students Extra Aid
content: Critics say a little-noticed provision would hurt the very constituencies — people of color, the working class, low-income Americans — that the party typically champions.
en_title: Democrats’ Bill Would Deny For-Profit College Students Extra Aid

title: Menace, as a Political Tool, Enters the Republican Mainstream
content: Threats of violence have become commonplace among a significant part of the party, as historians and those who study democracy warn of a dark shift in American politics.

title: Murkowski Announces Re-election Bid, Setting Up Clash With Trump
content: Of the seven Republicans who found former President Donald J. Trump guilty in his second impeachment trial, the Alaska senator is the only one facing re-election this year.
en_title: Murkowski Announces Re-election Bid, Setting Up Clash With Trump
en_content: Of the seven Republicans who found former President Donald J. Trump guilty in his second impeachment trial, the Alaska senator is the only one facing re-election this year.

title: Biden Chooses Robert Califf to Lead F.D.A., Despite Drug Industry Ties
content: Dr. Robert M. Califf, an academic and clinical trial researcher who ran the agency in 2016, has also consulted for pharmaceutical companies.

title: Why Democrats May Have a Long Wait if They Lose Their Grip on Washington
content: Voters’ reflexive instinct to check the party in power makes it hard for any party to retain a hold on both the White House and Congress for long.
en_title: Why Democrats May Have a Long Wait if They Lose Their Grip on Washington

title: Ocasio-Cortez Isn’t Wavering. Are New Yorkers on Her Side?

title: Political Campaigns Can Still Target You on Facebook

title: Problems at D.C. Jail Were Ignored Until Jan. 6 Defendants Came Along
content: At a hearing this week, officials said longstanding issues at the jail, where most inmates are Black, did not get much attention until the largely white rioters were held there.

title: Project Veritas and the Line Between Journalism and Political Spying

title: In Illinois, Dueling Infrastructure Votes Reflect Shifting G.O.P. Terrain
content: Representative Darin LaHood voted against the infrastructure bill while Representative Adam Kinzinger supported it. The split reflects the Republican Party’s post-Trump era of uncertainty.

en_content: Representative Darin LaHood voted against the infrastructure bill while Representative Adam Kinzinger supported it. The split reflects the Republican Party’s post-Trump era of uncertainty.

title: U.S. sues Uber, saying it discriminated against people with disabilities.
content: The Justice Department says Uber did not adjust its wait time fees for people with disabilities, even when Uber knew someone needed additional time.
